mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers the discount rate on future cash flows and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns over the next month turn positive when policy is still drifting down from prior restraint.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of months with declining FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 3-year rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and softer demand, which should depress equity returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations get revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 60-day forward returns more than half the time, or if the average forward return after the trigger is non-negative across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth and retail sales both accelerate together, the market is observing synchronized household income and spending strength, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through better revenue growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS signal does not outperform either series alone, or if positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of joint-strength observations over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above trend indicates current output is expanding without immediate slack, which supports cyclical equity earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO is above its 6-month moving average and rising for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger is followed by negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average excess return after the trigger is not positive in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in housing starts captures an early housing-demand break that often leads broader activity, so S&P 500 returns over the next quarter should weaken as cyclical growth expectations are repriced lower.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 8% year-over-year
failure_condition: Reject if year-over-year HOUST declines are not followed by negative 60-day returns on average, or if the signal's hit rate is below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation that remains elevated while the policy rate is near zero preserves real-rate repression and supports risk assets, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next month when CPI inflation stays above target and FEDFUNDS remains deeply accommodative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation above 2% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-plus-low-rate condition does not yield positive 20-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if the joint signal has no incremental value over CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: Employment growth that outpaces the unemployment rate signals labor-market absorption rather than weakness, which should lift equity returns over the following week by strengthening confidence in demand resilience.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-strength signal fails to produce positive 5-day returns in a majority of observations, or if rolling 2-year rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth below trend confirms a broad demand slowdown that should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as sales and margin expectations are revised lower.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth falls below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend GDP growth is not followed by negative 60-day returns on average, or if the signal fails to outperform a random baseline across any 4-year rolling window.
